title: Berrun

{{Quote|Finally tracked down my old squad-mates, master Jedi. Wishing I hadn't. General Garza recruited them into the Special Forces black ops program, but now they're in a detention facility on Berrun—convicted of war crimes.|[[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Fideltin Rusk]]|<ref name="The Surgeons" />}}
'''Berrun''' was a terrestrial astronomical object<ref name="Jedi" /> located in the [[Berrun system/Legends|Berrun system]], a part of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]].<ref name="Atlas" /> By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]], the Republic maintained a [[Berrun detention center|detention facility]] on Berrun. After being convicted of war crimes as a result of targeting the families of their Imperial targets, the members of the [[Republic Special Forces Division|Republic Special Forces]] [[Squad/Legends|squad]] known as the [[Surgeons]] served out their life sentences in the [[Prison/Legends|prison]].<ref name="The Surgeons" />

In [[34 BBY/Legends|34 BBY]],<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the events of ''[[Jedi Quest: The Dangerous Games]]'', which establishes that the [[Galactic Games/Legends|Galactic Games]] were held on Berrun seven [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior, in [[27 BBY]].</ref> Berrun hosted the [[Galactic Games/Legends|Galactic Games]], a sporting event that was held every seven [[Standard year/Legends|years]] in a different [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] location. The [[Euceron (species)|Euceron]] [[athlete]] [[Maxo Vista]] won five events at the Games on Berrun, earning him [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] fame.<ref name="Jedi" />

==Behind the scenes==
Berrun was first mentioned in the young readers novel ''[[Jedi Quest: The Dangerous Games]]'', written by [[Jude Watson]] and published in [[2002]] as the third volume in the ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Quest]]'' series.<ref name="Jedi" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Berrun [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Berrun itself, in grid square M-10.<ref name="Atlas" />
